senses.definition: a propeller that is shaped like a screw or uses a helical blade design to convert rotational motion into thrust
examples: The ship uses a screwpropeller for propulsion.
Older models relied on a single large screwpropeller to push the vessel forward.

senses.definition: plural of screw; a metal fastener with a helical ridge used to join objects by twisting
examples: Tighten the screws on the cabinet door.
The screws were rusted and needed replacing.

senses.definition: to twist or drive a screw into something or to tighten with a screwdriver
examples: He screws the lid on tightly.
She screws the plate to the wall.

senses.definition: slang for a difficult or troublesome problem or situation
examples: We’ve got a screws now with the budget cuts.
That project hit a real screw with supply delays.

senses.definition: a ship propelled by a screw propeller (also called a propeller ship)
examples: The screwship replaced the sailboat as the main cargo carrier.
Early screwships required powerful engines to move efficiently.

senses.definition: a person who works with screws; a worker who handles fasteners or mechanical assembly
examples: The screwsman was responsible for assembling the cases.
A skilled screwsman will choose the right thread for the job.

senses.definition: the stem or central shaft of a screw; a term seldom used outside technical contexts
examples: Inspect the screwstem for wear before reuse.
The screwstem must be aligned with the nut.

senses.definition: a quantity of screws kept in stock; a storage container or arrangement for screws
examples: Keep a screwstock of common sizes in the workshop.
The screwstock dwindled after the project.

senses.definition: a cap or lid that screws onto a bottle or container
examples: Tear off the plastic seal from the screwtop bottle.
The screwtop prevents leaks.

senses.definition: plural of screwtop; caps that screw onto containers
examples: The store offers glass bottles with screwtops.
Loose screwtops can cause leaks.

senses.definition: a mistake or blunder; a failure
examples: That naming screwup caused a lot of confusion.
Don’t make a screwup in the presentation.

senses.definition: to make a mistake or blunder
examples: He screwed up the math problem.
They screwed up the schedule again.

senses.definition: plural of screwup; multiple mistakes or blunders
examples: The project had several screwups this week.
He apologized for the screwups in the report.

senses.definition: a sarcastic or humorous expression involving screws; a nonsense term not widely used
examples: The phrase screwwise popped up in a quirky tech meme.
Few dictionaries include screwwise as a standard term.

senses.definition: a parasitic fly larva that bores into living tissue, causing screwworm disease
examples: Screwworm infestations were once common in warm climates.
Veterinarians treat animals to prevent screwworm outbreaks.

senses.definition: plural of screwworm; multiple screwworm larvae
examples: The farm implemented measures to control screwworms.
Screwworms can cause serious tissue damage.

senses.definition: slightly crazy or odd; behaving in a strange or unpredictable way
examples: That idea sounds a bit screwy.
His screwy plan might work if they had more time.

senses.definition: likely a typographical error or nonstandard term; not recognized in standard dictionaries
examples: The document contains scrfchar and needs editing.

senses.definition: capable of being written or inscribed
examples: The smooth surface is scribable with a pencil.
This material is not easily scribable.

senses.definition: fond of writing or inclined to write; writing-focused
examples: A scribacious student kept journals daily.
Her scribacious habits made her a prolific writer.

senses.definition: the quality of being scribacious; fondness for writing
examples: Her scribaciousness drove her to publish a collection of essays.
The club valued scribaciousness among its members.

senses.definition: relating to scribes or the act of writing; traditional or medieval in tone
examples: A scribal tradition preserved ancient texts.
The manuscript showed scribal quirks of the era.

senses.definition: plural of scribal; people who copy or write manuscripts; scribes
examples: The scribals of the monastery copied ancient texts.
Scribals maintained careful records for generations.

senses.definition: a nonstandard or miswritten term; not found in standard dictionaries
examples: The note included scribanne and several unfamiliar words.

senses.definition: fantastical or highly imaginative in writing; given to scribble-like progress in prose
examples: Her scribatious stories blend myth and science.
A scribatious style can be playful but challenging.

senses.definition: the quality of being scribatious; imaginative or fanciful writing
examples: His scribatiousness made the book a delightful read.

senses.definition: a short scribble or note; a small piece of writing
examples: He left a scribbet on the margin.
The teacher collected scribbets from the students.

senses.definition: a collection of scribbles or doodles; a tangle of writing
examples: Her notebook was filled with scribblage and sketches.
We organized the scribblage into a single page.
